findings.message: D7 states that concrete model values should live in `agent-profiles.json` and that non-canonical configuration examples should not be placed in the ADR body, but the preceding paragraph includes a concrete provider/model example (`claude-opus-4-7`, `gpt-5.4-mini`). Remove the concrete example or relax the stated rule; as written the ADR contradicts itself on whether model examples belong in the ADR.

T001 says to parse ADR front-matter with `serde_yaml::Value` and manually walk `decisions[].id`. That conflicts with the typed-deserialization rule for `libs/infrastructure/src/verify/`, which requires typed `Deserialize` DTOs instead of untyped Value walking in verify/guard code.

T003 makes `CatalogueSpecSignal.entry_hash` a required `ContentHash`, but T005 still requires decoding legacy `<layer>-catalogue-spec-signals.json` files without `entry_hash` and returning a `None`-equivalent. Those requirements cannot both be true as written; the codec needs either an explicit optional/migration wrapper or a non-optional field with a different legacy-handling rule.

T008 still permits a `todo!()`-style stub in `libs/infrastructure/src/ref_verify.rs`. That is a panic path in library code, which conflicts with the repo's no-panics rule and makes the adapter path unsafe if the stub is ever exercised.

The `RoundType` doc says `RefVerifierPort.verify_pair` takes `RoundType`, but `usecase-types.json` defines that port parameter as `domain::tddd::ModelTier`, and T008 explicitly says the adapter performs the `ModelTier -> RoundType` conversion. This catalogue entry is therefore stale and contradicts the layer boundary.

T008 makes the only planned `RefVerifierPort` implementation fail-closed after resolving provider/model, deferring the actual model invocation to a future stub. That contradicts the same plan's `ref-verify run`, cache-writing, and `check-approved` acceptance path, because no implementation task can ever produce `Pass` entries for the verify-cache artifacts.

`RefVerifierPort::verify_pair` returns a full `SemanticVerifyEntry` containing `claim_hash` and `evidence_hash`, but the port receives only raw `claim` and `evidence` strings plus a tier. IN-04 defines those hashes as externally determined identities, especially ADR git blob hash for Chain①, so the adapter cannot construct the required cache key correctly from this interface.

`CatalogueSpecSignal` is declared with a complete field list containing only `type_name`, `signal`, and `entry_hash`, but the spec and plan require the existing `catalogue_declaration_hash` to be retained and coexist with the new per-entry hash. Implementing this catalogue literally would remove the whole-file hash that CN-10 says must remain.

T007 requires the usecase layer to downgrade a citation-less Pass to Pending and even test that case, but the same artifacts make that state unrepresentable: `SemanticVerdict::Pass` requires `EvidenceCitation`, and `RefVerifierPort` returns `SemanticVerdict`. A citation-less pass cannot reach the interactor; this handling belongs in the adapter/codec boundary or should be removed from the usecase task.

T007 assigns `VerifySemanticRefsInteractor` responsibility for enumerating Chain pairs, reading existing verify-cache artifacts, skipping unchanged `(claim_hash, evidence_hash)` pairs, and writing updated cache artifacts, but the usecase catalogue only injects `Arc<dyn RefVerifierPort>`. There is no secondary port for spec/catalogue reads, cache persistence, hash source, or ref-verify config, so the plan either forces infrastructure I/O into the usecase layer or leaves the interactor unable to perform its stated workflow. Add explicit usecase ports/dependencies for these external operations or move the I/O responsibility out of the interactor plan.

The infrastructure catalogue declares `AgentRefVerifierAdapter::new` with only `profiles: Arc<AgentProfiles>`, while T008 says the adapter stores an agent execution client/runner and tests require `AgentRefVerifierAdapter::new` to receive both `AgentProfiles` and the runner. As written, the catalogue and implementation plan disagree on the adapter boundary, making the planned tests/implementation inconsistent. Include the runner/client dependency in the type catalogue constructor or revise T008 to explain how invocation is supplied without injection.

`RefVerifyError` documents `EscalationRequired` as human escalation, but the spec explicitly excludes immediate human escalation for known-bad-pair degradation and requires the D5 path to try the heavyweight model first before reporting to a human. T007 also says a below-threshold known-error detection test returns `EscalationRequired`, so an implementer following the catalogue would short-circuit the required段2 escalation path. Rename/scope the error to final human escalation only, or add a separate model-escalation state that preserves the D5 sequence.

T001 makes ADRs without YAML front matter skip anchor validation and pass, but AC-05 requires `plan-artifact-refs` to fail when `spec.json` contains an `AdrRef` with a non-existent anchor. With this exception, any anchored reference to a grandfathered ADR can never satisfy the stated existence check, so the plan can pass invalid anchors contrary to D3/AC-05. Either narrow AC-05 to front-matter ADRs or make missing front matter fail for anchored refs.

AC-06 says the per-entry hash must be added to catalogue entries and that the `<layer>-types.json` codec encodes/decodes it, but T003/T005 place `entry_hash` on `CatalogueSpecSignal` and update the `<layer>-catalogue-spec-signals.json` codec instead. That leaves the actual type catalogue entries without the hash AC-06 requires, so the implementation can complete the planned tasks while still not meeting the acceptance criterion for differential Chain② review.

T010 only adds `bin/sotp ref-verify check-approved` to the commit gate, while the spec requires the commit final gate to verify all Chains before commit and the plan defines `check-approved` as a cache-only reader. Without a commit-gate `bin/sotp ref-verify run` step, changed reference pairs after the phase gate are never semantically re-reviewed at the final gate, so the plan does not implement IN-02/D2's final verification step.

IN-11 requires both the public `/track:ref-verify` skill and the `bin/sotp ref-verify` subcommand, but the coverage map assigns IN-11 only to T007 and T009. T011 is the task that actually creates the `/track:ref-verify` skill and command file, so the current coverage lets IN-11 appear covered while omitting half of its required surface.

`CatalogueSpecSignal` declares only the updated `new` method here, but the implementation plan for T003 also requires an `entry_hash()` accessor and T005's tests consume that accessor. The type catalogue and plan therefore disagree on the public API needed for `entry_hash`, leaving implementers following the catalogue without the method required by the later codec task.

T007 skips unchanged `(claim_hash, evidence_hash)` pairs before fast-tier evaluation, but IN-08/AC-09 require known-bad pairs to be injected into each batch to monitor verifier degradation. As written, a stable known-bad probe with a cached entry is skipped, so later phase/commit runs can satisfy the cache rule without exercising the model or measuring detection rate. The plan needs to exempt known-bad probes from cache-hit skipping, or make them non-cacheable by design.

The cache port API cannot route results for `RefVerifyScope::All` into the required per-chain/per-layer cache artifacts. `save_entries(&cmd, Vec<SemanticVerifyEntry>)` receives a flat entry list, and each entry only carries hashes/verdict, while `All` covers Chain1 plus all Chain2 layers and IN-10/AC-03 require separate `spec-adr-verify-cache.json` and `<layer>-catalogue-spec-verify-cache.json` files. Without chain/layer identity on each pair/entry or explicit per-scope decomposition before saving, the commit-gate All scope is not executable.

`task-coverage.json` starts with `in_scope` mappings and has no `goal` section, so GO-01 through GO-03 are not mapped to any tasks even though the spec contains 43 elements including those goals. This leaves the top-level goals uncovered by the coverage artifact; add goal mappings or otherwise include GO-01/GO-02/GO-03 in task coverage.

IN-08 requires the known-bad injection rate and detection threshold to be configurable from a settings file with defaults, but task coverage maps IN-08 only to T007. T007 only introduces an injected `RefVerifyConfig` and explicitly keeps filesystem/config I/O out of the usecase; no CLI/composition task is assigned to load those settings or provide the 10%/90% defaults. As written, the plan cannot satisfy the spec's configurability requirement.

The spec requires `ref-verifier` to be independently configurable for model, timeout, and prompt, and AC-12 requires those settings not to interfere with `reviewer`. T006 only adds `provider`/`model`/`fast_provider`/`fast_model` and tests model resolution; T008 then formats the prompt in the adapter. There is no planned work for timeout or prompt configuration independence, so the plan only implements part of IN-09/AC-12.

T007 escalates only the known_bad probes when the fast-tier detection rate falls below threshold, then persists the production verdicts without rechecking the production pairs that were passed by the now-untrusted fast tier. That undercuts IN-08/AC-09's calibration gate: a batch-level monitor failure should cause the affected production work to enter the escalation path, otherwise the hard gate can cache false Pass verdicts from a verifier that just failed its known-bad check.

`AgentExecutionRunner` is declared as a `SecondaryPort` while its own docs and T008 describe it as an infrastructure-local runner abstraction used inside `AgentRefVerifierAdapter`. That places a hexagonal boundary port in the infrastructure layer for an adapter implementation detail; either make it a real usecase/domain port if it is part of the application boundary, or keep it as an infrastructure-internal trait and do not catalogue it as `SecondaryPort`.

T007's core flow says that when the known_bad detection rate falls below the threshold, the whole fast batch is re-run at final, including production pairs that already passed. That contradicts the same spec's IN-06/AC-08 requirement that only Fail/Pending pairs are escalated and that no batch-wide escalation occurs, while this task also claims to satisfy AC-08. As written, implementers cannot satisfy both the task and the acceptance criteria.

`RefVerifyConfig` is modeled as a value object but its percentage fields are plain `u8`s with no constructor or validated percentage type. T009 only says the CLI rejects out-of-range config, but this usecase-level config is injected directly and can still represent impossible states such as `known_bad_detection_threshold_percent = 255` or `max_parallelism = 0`, which can make the semantic gate fail or behave nonsensically outside the CLI path. The plan should make these settings validated at the type boundary, not only at one adapter.

T007 requires `RefVerifyPercent::try_new(0)` to succeed and uses the same percent type for both known-bad injection rate and detection threshold. That allows a config with 0% injection, so no known-bad probes are injected, or a 0% threshold, so degradation can never fall below the threshold. This contradicts IN-08/AC-09, which require known-bad probes to be injected into each batch and to trigger escalation when detection is below threshold. The plan should make the semantic health-check settings non-disableable, e.g. by validating injection and threshold as 1..=100 or by using separate field-specific value objects.

AC-08 states that only production pairs that Fail/Pending are escalated and that fast-pass pairs never run the final tier. That conflicts with the known_bad calibration path in AC-09/D5/D6: once the injected known_bad checks fall below threshold, the fast verifier for that batch has already been marked degraded, so caching same-batch fast-pass production verdicts without final re-evaluation (as T007 also specifies) can freeze false Passes despite the hard-gate intent.

`HumanEscalationRequired` is documented as the outcome when final-tier verification still has failed/pending production pairs. That collapses two D5 paths: verifier degradation/unresolved cases may require human escalation, but confirmed production failures are supposed to be handed back to the writer/fix loop and then reverified. As written, the usecase contract makes confirmed semantic failures look like human-escalation failures and does not represent the writer handback path.

T003 adds `entry_hash` to `CatalogueSpecSignal`, changes the constructor, and says all construction sites should pass the new value, but it explicitly defers `CatalogueSpecSignalsDocument` encode/decode changes to T005. Since the signals codec is one of the places that constructs/decodes `CatalogueSpecSignal`, the T003/T005 ordering leaves the plan unable to compile or verify at the T003 boundary. Merge the codec update into T003 or make T003/T005 a single executable task group.

T006/T008 require `timeout_seconds` and `prompt_template_path` to be added as typed fields on `CapabilityConfigDto` / `ResolvedExecution`, and say `resolve_execution("ref-verifier", ...)` returns those values. The infrastructure catalogue, however, only models `AgentProfiles` as a reference and keeps `resolve_execution` returning `Option<ResolvedExecution>` while exposing timeout/prompt as separate methods, without any `ResolvedExecution` or `CapabilityConfigDto` modification. That leaves the plan and catalogue with conflicting contracts for how the adapter obtains timeout/prompt, so the implementation can satisfy one artifact while violating the other.

IN-06 says only production pairs that Fail or Pending at the fast tier are escalated to final, but AC-08 requires fast-Pass production pairs in a degraded known_bad batch to be re-evaluated at final before caching. That leaves T007 with conflicting rules for the same path: either fast Passes are never escalated unless they Fail/Pending, or they must be escalated when calibration fails. The plan should state the known_bad degradation exception consistently in IN-06 or remove it from AC-08.

`RefVerifierPort::verify_pair` declares its tier parameter as `domain::tddd::ModelTier`, but the domain catalogue declares `ModelTier` under `tddd::semantic_verify` and the surrounding semantic types are referenced through `domain::tddd::semantic_verify::...`. This makes the cross-layer port contract inconsistent with the declared domain type catalogue and will steer T007/T008 toward a different or nonexistent type path instead of the dedicated semantic-review type.

The plan defines `RefVerifyPairSourcePort` and `RefVerifyCachePort` as infrastructure-backed ports for pair enumeration, known_bad injection, hash/cache routing, and artifact persistence, but the infrastructure catalogue only plans `AgentRefVerifierAdapter`. Since T007 delegates these responsibilities out of usecase and no concrete pair-source/cache adapters are planned, `bin/sotp ref-verify run` cannot be wired without adding unplanned infrastructure types/tasks.

T004 requires new verify-cache codec contracts (`SpecAdrVerifyCacheDocumentCodec::encode/decode`, `CatalogueSpecVerifyCacheDocumentCodec::encode/decode`, and DTO encode/decode behavior), but the infrastructure catalogue does not declare any codec type, function, or inherent impl for them. That leaves the AC-03 verify-cache codec work outside the catalogue/spec contract, so the plan artifacts are internally inconsistent: the implementation plan depends on contracts that the type catalogue cannot track or verify.
